content.full_text:
December 9, 1992

Ms. Jeanette Turvill  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street, Ste. 800  
Sacramento, California 95814

SUBJECT: MOKELUMNE BLUFFS SUBDIVISION - CONFLICT OF INTEREST

Dear Ms. Turvill:

First, let me apologize for the tardiness of this letter regarding the conversation that we had on the morning of November 4, 1992. To remind you of the situation, let me describe the circumstances and my position with Amador County.

A development proposal has come forth involving the creation of approximately 125 residential lots easterly of Pine Grove, California. My personal residence is approximately 1500 lineal feet from the nearest portion of the proposed development as the crow flies. The question arose as to whether or not I had a conflict of interest since my department, and I personally, recommend conditions of approval on developments in general. I was advised to contact your office as to my position in this matter.

In our conversation of November 4th you indicated that the foremost criteria for determining a potential conflict of interest are whether I own property within 2500 lineal feet of the project and, if so, will my property increase or decrease in value by $10,000 or more if the project is either approved or denied.

I determined that my property is within 2500 lineal feet of the project as indicated above. The second criterion is the increase or decrease in value. You suggested that I have the property appraised in the before and after condition. It just so happens that our property is currently listed with a local real estate firm that has made an appraisal of the property. I went to the broker of that firm and asked for his professional opinion as to any increase or decrease in the value of my property if the project is either approved or denied. Attached is a letter from that broker

indicating that no such increase or decrease is contemplated or realistic.

Should you have any questions on this matter, please feel free to contact me at 209/223-6429.

Very truly yours,

[handwritten signature]
Gary L. Caldwell
Director

/ae

Attachment

cc: John Hahn

To Whom it may concern,

I currently have property located at 13969 Dogwood Ct. listed for sale and owned by Mr. & Mrs. Gary Caldwell. It has been brought to my attention that a new subdivision Mokelumne Bluffs has parcel map approval and reservations maybe taken. It is my professional opinion that the presence of this development will not increase or decrease the value of my client's home which is currently listed at $124,900. Certainly a deviation of $10,000 either up or down is unrealistic.

If I can be of add'l assistance, please let me know.

Sincerely,
Keith DesVoignes
